---
title: "PEPPER Lab"
about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image: img/group-photo.jpg
  links:
    - text: "Google Scholar"
      url: "https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=QL5IkxkAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao"
    - text: GitHub
      icon: github
      url: "https://github.com/PEPPER-lab-HMC"
    - text: Email
      url: "mailto:jessicaguo@arizona.edu"

comments: false
---

# **PEPPER lab (Plant & Ecosystem Physiology, Potential, and Environment Lab)**

The PEPPER lab at Harvey Mudd College studies how plants respond physiologically to climate change and the impacts of those responses for the whole ecosystem. Methods include field and lab data collection, novel plant instrumentation, data harmonization, and Bayesian statistical modeling.

## Professor:

### Jessica S. Guo

![](img/Guo_2022.jpg){fig-align="center"}

Jessica Guo is a plant ecophysiologist and data scientist who studies plant-environment interactions under extreme climate, focusing on drylands subject to potentially lethal combinations of heat and drought. She blends her passion for reproducible workflows, interactive visualizations, and hierarchical Bayesian models with expertise in plant water relations.

#### Education

**Northern Arizona University** \| Flagstaff, AZ

*Ph.D.* in Biological Sciences \| 2019

*M.S.* in Biological Sciences \| 2013

**Arizona State University** \| Tempe, AZ

Certificate in Statistics \| 2015

**Columbia University** \| New York, NY

*B.A.* in Environmental Biology \| 2010

#### Appointments

**University of Arizona** \| Data Scientist \| September 2020 - present

**University of Utah** \| Postdoctoral Researcher \| July 2019 - August 2020
